recording_notes: MONITOR RECORDING — phone filming INSIGNIA monitor (NEW LOCATION — not the RCA monitor from V11-V14/V17/V21). Camera angle is from slightly above and right of the monitor, with the trainee's hand/arm partially visible at right holding the instrument. Pink/red foam block, Penrose drain, perforated white base. BLACK SUTURE — much easier to track than V21's white suture, significantly improving scoring confidence. HEAVY suture debris on the perforated board above the foam — many prior attempts evident (cut suture fragments scattered across the board). This indicates V22 is NOT the first attempt in this session. Resolution 1920x1080. Clearer image than RCA monitor recordings.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments already in field at drain. Left instrument from lower left, right instrument from right (trainee's gloved hand visible holding it). Curved needle with black suture at drain. White suture loop visible at drain. Heavy suture debris on perforated board above foam — many prior attempts evident.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: TIMING STARTS at approximately 3 seconds — instruments already entering field. Needle appears pre-loaded. DIFFERENT LOCATION from V21 (Insignia monitor, not RCA). Black suture used — much better visibility.

frame_analyses.description: Needle driving through drain. Both instruments at drain. Curved needle visible passing through tissue. Drain is being lifted significantly during the pass — good counter-traction by left instrument. White suture (the drain) being manipulated.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Active needle pass. Drain lifted for access — good technique. Aggressive but controlled needle driving.

frame_analyses.description: Continuing needle pass. Both instruments engaged at drain level. Needle being driven through second mark. Drain lifted substantially — vigorous but controlled manipulation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Still in placement at 20 seconds. Needle driving continues.

frame_analyses.description: Suture PLACED. Both dark suture ends clearly visible extending from drain — one going left (with a loop resting on the gauze), one going right. Instruments retracted from field. Clean view of placed suture through drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement complete at approximately 26-28 seconds (23-25s placement time from first instrument). Both ends visible. Good placement — suture appears through the mark area.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments re-entering field for first throw. Left instrument from left, right from right. Both dark suture ends visible extending from drain, widely splayed. Starting to position for first throw wrap.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transitioning to first throw. Short pause between placement and throw start.

frame_analyses.description: First throw wrapping in progress. Both instruments near drain. Suture loops visible — white loop at right side (suture being wrapped). Dark suture visible crossing at drain. Instruments working above drain level.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw wrap underway. 18+ seconds into first throw.

frame_analyses.description: First throw completed and tightened. Both instruments at drain level. Knot visible with dark suture X/cross pattern at drain center. Suture ends extending from knot. Instruments repositioning for second throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw complete at approximately 56-58 seconds (~28s duration). Knot seated at drain. Starting second throw.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw tightening. Left instrument from upper left pulling. Knot visible at center. Suture tails extending to lower right. Second throw being cinched down.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw completing at approximately 72-75 seconds (~15s duration). Efficient. Hand switch appears present.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw in progress. Both instruments at drain. Suture wrapping visible — loops of dark suture around drain area. Left instrument from upper left. Purple/colored highlight visible at instrument tip. Large suture loops.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw wrapping. Hand switch appears present from second throw.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw completing. Knot visible at drain center with dark suture crossing pattern. Tails extending left and right. Both instruments at sides. Drain appears well-approximated with knot seated and slit closing.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw completing at approximately 95-98 seconds (~22s duration). Transitioning to cutting.

frame_analyses.description: Early cutting phase. EXCELLENT VIEW of knot quality — dark suture X/cross pattern clearly visible at drain center. Drain slit appears WELL CLOSED around the knot with good approximation. The drain is slightly pinched (hourglass shape) showing good knot tension. Long suture tails extending — one to lower left, one curving down. Scissors at lower left beginning first cut.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting underway. 10+ seconds into cutting. The knot quality is clearly visible here — compact, centered, secure appearance. Drain closure appears BEST in recent videos.

frame_analyses.description: Mid-cutting. Instrument from upper left at drain. Knot visible. Tails shorter than at 108s — one short tail at upper left, one longer to right. Progress on cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 34+ seconds into cutting. Gradual progress. One tail appears partially cut.

frame_analyses.description: Final cuts completing. Both instruments at drain. Knot visible with SHORT stubs. Suture tails appear well-trimmed — much shorter than V20/V21 final frames. Drain slit well-closed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting phase completing at approximately 153-155 seconds. Tails are SHORT and well-trimmed — BEST cutting result in several videos.

frame_analyses.description: TASK COMPLETE. Instruments withdrawn. Clean final view: Compact knot at drain center with dark suture X pattern. SHORT well-trimmed suture tails (stubs only). Drain slit WELL CLOSED — excellent approximation around the knot. Drain slightly compressed (hourglass shape) confirming secure knot tension. Drain centered on block, NOT avulsed. No visible gap. Red-handled instrument (scissors?) visible resting on desk below monitor — confirms instruments withdrawn.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: BEST FINAL RESULT in 5+ videos. Short tails, good closure, compact knot, no gap, no avulsion. The combination of accurate placement, secure knot, well-closed drain, and short trimmed tails represents the highest quality completion since V14/V15.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw 28-58s (30s). Longest throw — includes some repositioning time. Double wrap visible consistent with surgeon's knot. Knot seated well. Black suture easier to track. Confidence: moderate.
Second throw 58-75s (17s). Efficient. Single throw. Hand switch appears present. Confidence: low-moderate.
Third throw 75-98s (23s). Moderate pace. Hand switch appears present. Knot well-secured after this throw — drain compression visible. Confidence: low-moderate.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Black suture through drain visible at 28s. Both ends exit drain at appropriate positions. The dark suture against white drain makes placement area more visible than V21. Marks not independently confirmed but suture passes through correct general area. Conservative 1.5mm per mark estimate.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: BEST drain closure assessment in recent series. At 108s, drain slit clearly well-closed with hourglass compression from knot tension. At 161s final frame, slit remains well-closed with compact centered knot. No visible gap. Short trimmed tails. Drain not avulsed, centered on block. Confidence: moderate — black suture and Insignia monitor provide better visibility than recent RCA recordings.

technique_summary: V22 at 152 seconds is a SIGNIFICANT IMPROVEMENT and the best performance since V15 (142s). This is recorded at a NEW LOCATION (Insignia monitor, different from RCA setup), with BLACK suture providing much better visual tracking. Heavy suture debris indicates multiple prior attempts in this session, yet the trainee still performed well — suggesting either good fatigue management or the benefits of warming up. Suture placement at 20 seconds is EXCELLENT. Throws at 70 seconds total are mid-range but effective. Cutting at 57 seconds remains the dominant bottleneck (37% of total time) but CRITICALLY, the QUALITY of cutting is much improved — the final frame shows SHORT, well-trimmed tails, the best cutting outcome in 5+ videos. The overall result quality (compact knot, well-closed drain, short tails, no gap, no avulsion) is the best since V14/V15. This video suggests the trainee benefits from practicing at this alternate location and/or with black suture.
improvement_suggestions: CUTTING TIME at 57 seconds still dominates (37% of total). However, the QUALITY of cuts (short tails) is dramatically improved — the trainee successfully cut close to the knot. This is progress.
First throw at 30 seconds is the longest phase in the throws — includes repositioning time. Tighter transitions from placement to first throw could save 5-10 seconds.
Consider whether this Insignia monitor setup or the RCA setup produces better training outcomes — the different location may offer advantages.
Black suture provides much better visibility for AI scoring — use consistently if available.

If this followed prior attempts in the session (heavy debris visible), the trainee should note which attempt number this was. If #2 or #3, the 152s time is strong. If #4+, fatigue management has improved.
Continue the improvement trajectory — 152s is approaching V15 territory (142s) and re-establishing the path toward V14 peak (107s).
strengths: BEST TIME in 7 videos: 152 seconds — faster than V15 (142s)? No, V15 was 142s. But 152s is the best since V15.
BEST CUTTING QUALITY in 5+ videos — short, well-trimmed tails in final frame
BEST DRAIN CLOSURE assessment in recent series — well-closed, hourglass compression, no visible gap
Suture placement excellent at 20 seconds — near series peak
Compact, centered knot with good tension
All three throws completed correctly with apparent hand switches
Drain not avulsed — perfect tissue handling record across 22 videos
Performed well despite heavy prior-attempt debris suggesting mid-session attempt
Black suture choice improves both real performance (visibility) and AI scoring confidence

phase_comparison.placement_20s: Excellent — 2s faster than V21 (22s), near series peak (22s V14). Placement is now consistently strong.
phase_comparison.throws_70s: Mid-range total. V14 peak: 48s. First throw at 30s is the drag. Second (17s) and third (23s) are efficient.
phase_comparison.cutting_57s: Time still high (37% of total). BUT quality is DRAMATICALLY improved — short trimmed tails vs V20/V21 long tails. This is the key breakthrough.

key_observations: 152s / 443 FLS — BEST performance since V15 (142s/452). 66 seconds faster than V20 crash.
NEW LOCATION: Insignia monitor, different setup from RCA lab. May provide training advantages.
BLACK SUTURE — easier to track, improves scoring confidence to 0.48 (from V21's 0.38).
Cutting time 57s still the bottleneck BUT cutting QUALITY is best in 5+ videos — short trimmed tails.
Heavy debris = mid-session attempt, yet performance is strong — better fatigue management or warmup benefit.
23% improvement from baseline (198s → 152s) — strongest improvement metric since V15.
Drain closure BEST assessment in recent series — well-closed with visible hourglass compression.
First throw at 30s is the slowest individual phase — optimization target.
V21→V22 improvement: 170s→152s (18s faster). Positive trajectory restored.
22nd video: trainee is back on the improvement path after the V18-V20 crash.
Fresh-session baseline concern from V21 partially addressed — 152s shows the trainee CAN still perform near peak when conditions are right.
